title: Blotterbook — improvement backlog (paste-ready prompts)
intro: From the 2026-06-25 audit. Each item is a self-contained prompt: scope, goal, constraints, "done when".
tags: Tags: P1 do-soon · P2 durable win · P3 polish. Send them one at a time (one surface/concern per turn).
note: Committed source of truth for the ACTIVE engineering backlog (open + guardrail items; replaces the retired .claude/backlog.md). Completed (Done) items are archived in data/backlog_archive.json to keep this file lean — the admin Backlog panel reads BOTH and merges them, so done items stay visible. The admin panel shows category/effort/status only; prompt + doneNote stay in the JSON.
commitConvention: Conventional-commit titles (enables CH12 automated versioning): when committing, and ESPECIALLY when titling a PR / squash-merge, lead the title with a conventional-commit type so the version bump can be derived automatically — `feat:` (new capability → minor), `fix:` (bug fix → patch), `chore:`/`refactor:`/`docs:`/`style:`/`test:`/`perf:` (→ patch), and append `!` (e.g. `feat!:`) or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer for a breaking change (→ major). Scope it when useful, e.g. `feat(staging): …`. Untyped/legacy titles default to a patch bump. Keep the existing Co-Authored-By and Claude-Session trailers on the commit body.
donePolicy: When an item's status is set to `done`, delete its `prompt` field and keep only the `doneNote` — the doneNote is the shipped record of what was actually done, so the original prompt is redundant and only bloats this file (the admin backlog panel never renders done-item prompts anyway). Keep `prompt` on `open` and `guardrail` items, since those are still the actionable spec. Applies retroactively: a done item should never carry a prompt. RECURRING ITEMS: an item with `recurring: true` (e.g. R1 repo audit, CH16 staging→prod promotion) is a perpetual driver — NEVER set its status to done, set completedDate, or strip its prompt; it stays open across every run and its prompt leads with a RECURRING/REPEATABLE marker. Each run ships its output as new items, not by closing the recurring item. ARCHIVE (post-split): completed items now live in `data/backlog_archive.json`. When you set an item to `done` and strip its prompt, MOVE the item object out of backlog.json into backlog_archive.json (the admin panel merges both). Recurring items never move — they stay open in backlog.json.

items.title: Per-file CSV storage / provenance (redesign CSV Library)
items.prompt: Design decision pending, then build. The redesigned CSV Library (src/app/screens/CsvLibrary.svelte, staging) currently defers per-file storage: the Store keeps only the merged trade set (no file provenance), so the screen shows one derived "active dataset" row and the importer just parses -> addTrades. Decide how per-file history should work, then implement it so the Library can list each imported CSV with its own metadata (name/label, platform, row+trade counts, coverage range, imported date, size, overlap/dup count vs the existing set), an include/exclude toggle that adds or removes just that file's trades, rename, re-import, download-original, and per-file delete. Open design questions to settle FIRST (ask the user): (a) store the raw CSV text/blob per file, or just a file record + which trade ids it contributed?; (b) how to attribute already-merged trades to a file (a fileId stamped on each Trade at import — needs a Trade-shape + tradeId consideration since the id is a content hash), and how to handle a trade that appears in two files (overlap); (c) IndexedDB size budget for storing raw CSVs. Constraints: keep everything behind the Store interface (A4) — add a `files` object store + methods, don't touch indexedDB directly from components; demo stays non-mutating; the pure-logic core (adapters/compute) is unchanged. Done when: the CSV Library lists real per-file records with working include/rename/delete/re-import, imported trades carry file provenance, and the aggregate dashboard still reconciles. — UPDATE 2026-07-01 (backlog review): post-CH16 the CSV Library ships on ALL surfaces (the '(staging)' framing is stale); the design decision (store per-file provenance vs keep the merged-set model) is unchanged and still pending. — DECIDED 2026-07-04 (owner): (a) STORE THE RAW CSV TEXT per file (enables re-import/download-original/future re-parse); (b) overlap trades carry a `fileIds` ARRAY (attributed to every contributing file); (c) 50 MB total raw-CSV budget with a user-facing warning as it approaches. Design questions are settled — this item is now build-ready and should be implemented TOGETHER with A208 (both revise the Trade shape + Store in one migration).

items.title: Keep the Store interface as the only persistence seam
items.prompt: Guardrail for future work: all reads/writes stay behind `Store` (`src/lib/core/store.ts`) so the planned cloud/ sync tier can drop in a `CloudStore` with the same async methods. Reject any change that calls `indexedDB` directly from app/component code.

items.title: Second full repo audit
items.prompt:
RECURRING — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done, set completedDate, or strip this prompt. Each run is a fresh full audit (this is the Nth, not a one-shot); the findings ship as NEW backlog items, and R1 itself stays open for the next pass. Read-only audit (NO code edits) of the current repo — architecture, code quality, duplicated code, and security posture — accounting for the CURRENT architecture (ADR-001) AFTER the Svelte/TypeScript reorg. Specifically: a Vite multi-page build (vite.config.mjs + the vite-ssg.mjs plugin) to `dist/` (A26), served by Cloudflare Pages + `/functions/*` edge functions (TypeScript, A78). The source tree is `src/{lib,app,site}` (A30): src/lib/ is the framework-agnostic pure-logic core in native TS (A61) — core.ts (compute/cost/format/event bus/pure helpers), adapters.ts, store.ts, demostore.ts, curveseries.ts, report.ts, sampledata.ts, format.ts, types.ts — reused verbatim per A29; src/app/ is the Svelte 5 SPA on ALL app surfaces after the A33 cutover (app/demo/staging are marker-free mount points loading src/app/main.ts -> App.svelte; the sidebar-shell App.svelte + screens/ + parts/ (src/app/components/ was deleted in the CH16 cutover); app-only glue in src/app/lib/; the vanilla view layer was deleted); src/site/ is the marketing/info Svelte SSG (A69 — components prerendered by vite-ssg.mjs, hydrated in place). Constraints to verify against: runes-only Svelte 5 (no `export let`/`$:`/createEventDispatcher/svelte-store writables), `src/` is `any`-free TS, the mode-aware Store seam via context('bb:store') (real IndexedDB for app/staging, in-memory DemoStore for demo — demo NEVER persists or mutates), `style-src 'self'` CSP (S18/A55 — no inline styles), design tokens only in src/styles/tokens.css, .ts is linted (A79) and typecheck = tsc(core) + tsc(functions) + svelte-check(app), and two-track automated versioning (CH12, scripts/bump-version.mjs). THE hard product constraint: compute is local — no trade data ever leaves the browser. Give concrete, prioritized findings (P1/P2/P3 with file:line), and append any new actions to this backlog in the same format. Done when: a written, prioritized audit is delivered and new follow-ups are added to the backlog.

— UPDATED 2026-06-30 (canonical shadcn-svelte re-platform, ADR-002): audit against the CURRENT architecture — a conventional Svelte 5 (runes) + Tailwind v4 + canonical shadcn-svelte app. Tokens: the shadcn semantic set (background/foreground/card/primary/secondary/muted/accent/destructive/border/ring + chart-1..5) live in `src/styles/tailwind.css` (the single source; `tokens.css` is DELETED). UI primitives are canonical shadcn-svelte at `src/lib/components/ui/` (bits-ui v2, added via the CLI) — the old in-house `src/ui`/`$ui` are GONE. Structure: `$lib` → `src/lib`, with the pure-logic core at `src/lib/core/`, `cn` at `src/lib/utils.ts`. Most component styling is now Tailwind utilities (scoped `<style>` only for genuinely bespoke bits). See ADR-002.

— RUN 2026-07-01 (post-CH16 full audit): delivered. Repo in strong shape — NO P1. Verified CLEAN: no trade-data egress, CSP style-src 'self' holds (zero inline style=), functions auth hardened (constant-time compare + Access JWT + fail-closed), Stripe webhook inert+signature-verified, backup/screenshot restore sanitized (validShot/importAll), src/ any-free + runes-only, no cutover orphans/dangling imports, demo non-mutation holds (every dashboard write isDemo-guarded + DemoStore in-memory), Store seam only, version-bump/tsconfig/vite/playwright/eslint all current, no unused deps, no orphaned scripts. FIXED INLINE this run: shared money()/usd() from core across the 7 screens (had drifted); removed MOCK_* fixtures from the shipped screens + retired the redundant /dev/app.html RedesignApp harness (de-mocked, props required); disabled Trade Editor + CSV-import controls on demo (UI affordance to match the guards); doc/comment fixes (CLAUDE.md modal.ts + dev/screens + UI-workflow, core.ts orientation, screen 'on staging' comments, test-version.mjs stale path, ci.yml step name, .prettierignore/tsconfig.functions cosmetics). Deferred → CH35 (optional CI parallelism).

— RUN 2026-07-01 pass 5 (post-#92): delivered (docs/repo-audit-2026-07-01-pass5.md). Baseline green (tests/lint/typecheck/format/audit clean; bundle 517/600 KiB); moat verified intact (no egress, CSP holds, functions auth solid); all house-rule constraint greps clean. This pass audited feature-level behavior and found 1xP1 + 6xP2 user-visible functional bugs (mostly CH16 mock leftovers/lost seams): dead onboarding CSV CTA (A147), workspace-save-[] blanks dashboard (A148), Blotter dead write controls (A149), Calendar ISO-week mislabels (A150), zero-emitter event bus so Activity Terminal never populates anywhere (A151, corrects A133), missing Calendar lightbox dialog (A152), incomplete tag canonicalization/migration (A153). P3s: CSV-export formula hardening + importAll residuals (A154), verifyAccessJwt exp/iss (A155), Reports export ignores meta (A156), duplication batch (A157), dead-code prune (A158), unreachable tag filter (A159), CsvLibrary fake dropzone (A160), gate gaps (A161), untyped fetch boundaries (A162), docs drift (A163), #92 cosmetic regressions (A164).

— METHOD UPDATE 2026-07-01 (post pass 5): audit BEHAVIOR by default, not just static constraints. Pass 5 proved the hygiene gates (greps/typecheck/lint) catch zero functional bugs — all seven P1/P2s were controls that rendered but did nothing when clicked. Each future pass should (a) exercise every screen's visible controls (the no-dead-controls e2e sweep in e2e/no-dead-controls.spec.mjs automates the baseline — keep its allow-list honest and extend it to new screens/controls), (b) diff each screen's advertised affordances (copy, icons, buttons) against what the wiring actually does, and (c) verify any state written by one path is readable by every path that displays it (the A130/A153 tag-form class).

items.title: Guardrail: verify Stripe webhook signature before provisioning
items.prompt: Guardrail for the payments scaffold: never provision an account/entitlement in `functions/api/webhook.ts` before verifying the Stripe signature — HMAC-SHA256 over the RAW request body with `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, constant-time compare, and a timestamp/replay window. The endpoint is an inert 501 stub today; reject any future change that wires up provisioning without signature verification first.

items.title: Promote Staging to Prod
items.prompt:
REPEATABLE — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done, set completedDate, or strip this prompt. Run to promote staging-only feature(s) onto prod + demo. Since the A33 cutover all three surfaces (app/demo/staging) are ONE Svelte SPA under src/app/ — there is no separate staging codebase to copy. Staging-only behavior lives behind `isStaging` / `PAGE_MODE === 'staging'` runtime guards INSIDE the shared components (the old STAGING_PAGE export is retired); promotion = removing those guards so the feature ships on every surface.

SCOPE: promote the feature(s) named in the request; if none are named, promote the full staging delta (staging is a proving ground — every feature is destined for prod + demo). Inventory with `grep -rn "isStaging\|PAGE_MODE === 'staging'" src/app src/lib` and CLASSIFY each hit: FEATURE gate (promote) vs ENVIRONMENT infra (leave) — the environment set (isolated IndexedDB + seed via SEEDED, the per-surface storage key namespacing MOD_KEY/TABS_KEY/WS_KEY, backup filename, env pill/version track, the onboarding gate, the key-gate in functions/_middleware.ts) stays put. List each feature + its gate(s) before editing.

PER FEATURE: (1) flip the gate to unconditional — e.g. `isStaging ? a : b` -> `a`, `{#if isStaging}` -> unconditional, `class:staging={isStaging}` + `.overlay.staging` CSS -> `.overlay`; remove the now-unused STAGING_PAGE import. (2) DEMO 1:1 — the feature must work on demo, but keep demo NON-MUTATING: any TRADE-writing control stays `disabled` under `isDemo` and guarded (`if (isDemo) return;`), and the in-memory DemoStore means UI/layout prefs (saved layouts, hidden modules) simply don't persist — that's correct, not a bug. (3) keep it CSP-clean (no inline style="" — A55) and a11y-intact.

THEN: `npm test` (lint+typecheck+format+unit) + `npm run test:e2e` (boots all surfaces). UPDATE e2e: any spec asserting demo/prod LACKS the feature (e.g. 'demo … no menu', 'demo … not paginated') must flip to assert it now HAS it. Verify in a browser (build -> serve dist/ -> Playwright) that the demo + app surfaces show the feature. Title the PR `feat:` (or `fix:`) so CH12 bumps prod by the right level — do NOT hand-edit data/versions.json (staging keeps its lead). Add a data/changelog.json (F13) entry at the top of `releases`, keyed to the NEW prod version, describing what shipped for users. The bump-version STAGING_ONLY set is just `src/app/staging.html` (the env page) — features were never in it, so no change there.

Each run done when: the in-scope features are live on prod + demo at 1:1 (demo non-mutating), all surfaces verified, npm test + e2e green, a changelog entry added, committed/pushed (PR only if asked). Leave this item OPEN (repeatable).

— UPDATED 2026-07-01 (UI-redesign Phase 3 cutover): the promotion model above (flip STAGING_PAGE guards inside the shared App.svelte components) describes the PRE-redesign world and now applies ONLY to the small set of legacy staging features still gated that way (grep `STAGING_PAGE src/` — Overview/TradeBlotter/AdvancedStats). The DOMINANT staging delta is now the WHOLE redesigned sidebar-shell app: `src/app/main.ts` dynamically mounts `src/app/StagingApp.svelte` (+ `src/app/screens/*`, `src/app/lib/dashboard.svelte.ts`, `src/lib/components/shell/*`) for `PAGE_MODE==='staging'` ONLY, while app/demo still mount the legacy `src/app/App.svelte`. So the big promotion is a CUTOVER — point app/demo at the redesigned root too (drop the `PAGE_MODE==='staging'` branch in main.ts and retire App.svelte + its now-unreachable STAGING_PAGE branches) — NOT a per-gate flip. That cutover is a large, owner-gated milestone; scope it explicitly and confirm demo stays non-mutating (DemoStore) before running it. The env infra (isolated IndexedDB, seed, LS namespacing, _middleware key-gate) still keys off PAGE_MODE and stays put.

— UPDATED 2026-07-01 (the big cutover ran): this run promoted the ENTIRE redesigned sidebar-shell app to prod + demo. The legacy `src/app/App.svelte` + its whole `src/app/components/*` view layer (19 components) and the `src/app/lib/modules.ts` A108 module registry were RETIRED (deleted); the redesigned `src/app/StagingApp.svelte` was RENAMED to `src/app/App.svelte` and is now THE single mode-aware app root; `src/app/main.ts` mounts it on ALL surfaces (no more staging-only dynamic-import branch), mode-aware via PAGE_MODE (app → real IndexedDB, no seed, empty → first-run onboarding; demo → seeded in-memory DemoStore, every write isDemo-guarded so demo never persists; staging → isolated seeded DB). The `STAGING_PAGE` export was removed from core.ts (the app derives an `isStaging` local from PAGE_MODE). Full prod parity was built on the redesigned screens/parts: editable cost setup, first-run onboarding, saved views, workspace layouts, per-trade screenshots, day-note tags, backup/restore/erase, activity terminal, definitions, and status banners — with demo kept non-mutating throughout. Docs synced (ui-redesign.md/CLAUDE.md/architecture.md). Remaining recurring scope for future runs: promote any genuinely staging-only feature added AFTER this cutover. — INVENTORY 2026-07-01: the current staging-only feature awaiting promotion is A135 (tabbed dashboards, gated behind isStaging in App.svelte + DashTabs.svelte). Promote once proven on staging. — GATING MODEL (A111 decision, docs/staging-gating-recommendation.md): runtime PAGE_MODE/isStaging gating IS the staging model (one bundle, promotion = deleting the gate). Staging gates are UX switches, NOT security boundaries — anything genuinely private belongs behind the server-side middleware. When a staging-only feature exceeds ~10 KiB minified or pulls a new dependency, load it behind {#if isStaging}{#await import(...)} so it stays out of the prod boot payload.

items.title: Discussion & planning: Trade Replay (simulated market snapshots)
items.prompt: Scope the effort + requirements for a Trade Replay feature that lets users step through simulated market snapshots in time around a trade to review it for analysis. Cover: what data is needed (do current platform exports carry intra-trade price/time, or only fills? what extra market data would replay require and from where?), storage/perf implications for a client-side static-hosted app, the UI surface, and a phased build estimate. Done when: a written feasibility/effort estimate exists with concrete next-step items and open questions.

items.title: Discussion & planning: economic calendar data on the calendar module
items.prompt: Scope adding economic-event data (FOMC and other standard futures-relevant events) to the calendar module, user-togglable, with ~5 years of historical events backed in. Assess: data sourcing/licensing, how/where the dataset is stored and shipped (static JSON? payload size for 5 years?), how events render on the calendar without crowding the existing trade/journal data, the toggle UX, and ongoing updates/maintenance. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements + next-step items + open questions exist.

items.title: Discussion & planning: Stripe integration (donations now, subscriptions later)
items.prompt: Scope the effort + requirements to set up Stripe. NEAR-TERM goal: accept donations — the user clicks/interacts with the pricing module on the homepage to trigger a Stripe donation flow. Cover: Stripe primitives for one-off donations (Payment Links vs Checkout Session vs Payment Element), what server side is needed on Cloudflare Pages (a Pages Function for the Checkout session + webhook, secret/key handling — no keys in the static client), the homepage UX hook on the existing pricing element, and test vs live mode. ALSO lay down scaffolding for monthly SUBSCRIPTIONS (Products/Prices, customer + billing portal, webhooks) even though subscriptions are a later goal gated on remote data storage + user accounts — note the dependency and what can be stubbed now vs deferred. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements (donations MVP path, subscription scaffolding, server/secret needs, open questions) + concrete next-step backlog items exist.

items.title: Add a copyright + operator line to the site footer
items.prompt: From R7. The shared site footer (src/site/lib/Footer.svelte — partials/footer.html was retired by A69) has no copyright line and never names the operating entity. Add a '© 2026 <name>' line (and, on legal.html / src/site/components/Legal.svelte, a 'Blotterbook is operated by <entity>' note). The exact entity display name is a pending owner decision (TBD as of R7 — 'Scaline Audio' per LICENSE); ship brand-only '© 2026 Blotterbook' now if undecided and swap in the entity once chosen. Since A69 the footer is a Svelte component prerendered by scripts/vite-ssg.mjs, so `npm run build` regenerates every page's footer automatically (no build-includes step). Done when: every page footer shows a copyright line and the operator is named once the entity is decided.

items.title: Pre-monetization legal pack: ToS, Privacy Policy, Donations/Refund policy
items.prompt: From R7 — prerequisite for R15 (Stripe). Before taking money, expand the legal content (legal.html / new sections or pages): (1) a real Terms of Service distinct from the current disclaimer bullets, with a governing-law/venue clause set to DELAWARE; (2) a standalone full Privacy Policy (today it's a summary) covering local-only storage, the coarse-region tax lookup, and any Stripe data once payments land; (3) a Donations/Refund policy stating the $25/$50 tiers are voluntary NON-REFUNDABLE donations with no goods/services exchanged; (4) name the operating entity / seller identity (BLOCKED on the owner's entity decision from R7). Keep the existing not-a-broker/not-advice disclaimers. Link the new docs from the footer and from the donation/checkout flow. Done when: ToS (Delaware), Privacy Policy, and the non-refundable Donations/Refund policy are published and linked, and the seller identity is shown. — UPDATE 2026-07-02 (backlog review): A181 (2026-07-02) audited the current legal/privacy copy against the app's real behavior and fixed the drift (outbound-call inventory, storage disclosure) — build the formal pack on that corrected baseline; its doneNote lists the accounts-era GDPR/CCPA preconditions.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Rate-data scraper must stay within the 50-subrequest free-tier cap
items.prompt: Guardrail for the roadmap's scheduled rate-data scraper (the Cron Worker that will pull CME fee schedules, per-broker data-feed prices, and per-state tax tables into `data/*.json`). The Workers FREE plan allows only 50 EXTERNAL subrequests per invocation. When this is built, design within that cap: batch/limit outbound fetches to <=50 per run (count the GitHub commit-back call), split sources across multiple scheduled invocations, or move to Workers Paid. Reject a single-invocation design that fans out to dozens of sources on the free tier.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Move to Workers Paid before shipping the paid cloud-sync tier
items.prompt: Guardrail for the planned cloud-sync subscription tier (the `CloudStore` behind the `Store` seam, A4). Every device push/pull is a Pages Function invocation against the Workers FREE plan's 100,000 requests/day cap — a few hundred active sync users would exhaust it — and KV's 1,000 writes/day free cap is far too low for user data. Do NOT ship a paying sync product on the free Workers plan: treat the Workers Paid plan ($5/mo — 10M requests + higher KV/CPU) as a prerequisite, with R2 (10 GB free, free egress — fits the zero-knowledge model) for encrypted trade blobs and D1/KV for accounts/entitlements. Plan the economics around Workers Paid (trivial against subscription revenue), not around stretching the free tier. Reject a sync rollout that assumes the free request/KV caps.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Keep Workers KV low-write — never store per-visitor state in KV
items.prompt: Guardrail for KV usage (`STATUS_KV`). The Workers FREE plan caps KV at 1,000 WRITES/day (plus 1,000 deletes + 1,000 lists; reads are 100k/day; 1 GB storage). Today KV holds only admin-set status + feature-flag config and the best-effort admin rate-limiter — all low-write and fail-open, which is fine. Keep it that way: never put per-VISITOR or per-REQUEST state in KV (counters, analytics, sessions, public-endpoint rate-limiting) — that would blow 1,000 writes/day almost immediately and start erroring. New per-user/per-request persistence belongs in D1/R2 (and on Workers Paid). Reject a change that writes to KV on a public or high-traffic path.

items.title: Guardrail: admin-endpoint security must not depend on the fail-open rate limiter
items.prompt: From the R1 audit. rateLimited() (functions/_lib/http.ts) returns false (ALLOW) whenever STATUS_KV is unbound and is best-effort/non-atomic; it is the only throttle on /api/status, /api/config, and /api/admin-key (token issuance, 20/60s). This is the correct trade-off (don't lock the admin out; endpoints sit behind Access + constant-time token/key compare), so it is defense-in-depth only. Guardrail: never let an endpoint's security DEPEND on rateLimited() — auth (Access JWT + constant-time compare) is the real control; reject any future endpoint that relies on the limiter as primary protection, and keep STATUS_KV bound in prod so the throttle is actually active. Adjacent: A17 (KV low-write), S11 (webhook signature).

items.title: (guardrail) Dev tooling stays dev-only — SUPERSEDED IN PART by ADR-001/A28
items.prompt: PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md. This guardrail was written for the R19 Tier A world where the shipped app was zero-dep and the committed files were the deploy artifact (so devDeps could never touch shipped bytes, and SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL=1 was set on Pages). ADR-001 reverses that: Tier B (Vite build) is being adopted, so build deps now DO produce shipped bytes and Pages WILL install + build. The replaced clauses: 'shipped output stays dependency-free', the drift gate as proof-of-no-shipped-deps, and SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL — those no longer hold after A26 lands. WHAT STILL APPLIES until/after migration: keep node_modules + build output (playwright-report, test-results, the Vite dist/output dir) gitignored; pin every dep with a committed lockfile. The NEW dependency discipline lives in A28 (minimal/pinned/audited; supply chain as a security control; local-compute pillar gates every dep). Reference A28, not this item, going forward.

items.title: (guardrail) Dependency policy — pinned/lockfiled/audited supply-chain hygiene (minimalism retired, ADR-002)
items.prompt:
Guardrail from ADR-001 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md), replacing the zero-runtime-dep stance (A25). Runtime/build dependencies are now ALLOWED, under discipline — because for a privacy product handling financial data, the supply chain IS a security control. (1) EARN-ITS-WEIGHT: add a dep only when hand-rolling it is materially worse; keep writing small utilities by hand (small helpers). Heavyweight enablers qualify (the framework, charting like lightweight-charts, layout/virtualization libs); date/utility micro-libs do not. (2) SUPPLY CHAIN: pin every version + commit the lockfile; run `npm audit` in CI; apply Subresource Integrity / strict CSP to shipped bundles; keep the dependency surface minimal; consider vendoring the few critical libs so a registry compromise can't silently reach users. (3) LOCAL-COMPUTE PILLAR GATES EVERY DEP (hard): reject any dep or dep feature that phones home, ships telemetry/analytics, or could exfiltrate trade data — no exceptions, regardless of convenience. Reject any dependency addition that fails the earn-its-weight bar or the local-compute gate.

— UPDATED 2026-06-30 (canonical shadcn-svelte re-platform, ADR-002): the decision owner RETIRED the dependency-minimalism / earn-its-weight posture — Blotterbook now adopts dependencies freely as a conventional Svelte 5 + Tailwind v4 + shadcn-svelte app (the shadcn-svelte CLI is the component workflow; bits-ui/tailwind-variants/tw-animate-css/etc. ship). What REMAINS as a guardrail is supply-chain HYGIENE only: pin versions + commit the lockfile + `npm audit` in CI + keep CSP `style-src/script-src 'self'`. Drop the "minimal / no-runtime-dep / hand-roll it" language when citing A28.

items.title: (guardrail) Preserve the pure-logic core verbatim through the framework migration
items.prompt:
Guardrail from ADR-001 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md). The Svelte migration (A27 + later Phase 4) must touch the VIEW LAYER ONLY. The pure-logic modules are the hard-won, test-covered value and must be imported into components VERBATIM, not rewritten: src/lib/core/adapters.ts (platform CSV adapters + detection + pairFills), compute() (metrics), costModel() (commissions/subscriptions/tax/take-home), the Section-1256 tax model, src/lib/core/store.ts (the Store seam — A4), and src/lib/core/format.ts. They stay framework-agnostic so the node test suites (test-adapters/test-version/test-auth/test-flags/test-tax) keep covering them unchanged. Reject a framework PR that reimplements adapter parsing, the metrics/cost/tax math, or persistence inside a component instead of importing the existing module. The Store interface (A4) remains the only persistence seam. TYPESCRIPT CLARIFICATION (A61, docs/archive/typescript-decision.md): converting the core from JSDoc-typed .js to native .ts (folded into the A30 reorg) is PERMITTED — it changes the syntax host (.js→.ts, JSDoc→inline types), NOT the algorithms. 'Verbatim' here means NO logic rewrite, not 'never touch the file'. The gate is that the node suites pass IDENTICALLY before/after the conversion (they are the behavior-preserving safety net). Still reject any change that reimplements the parsing/metrics/cost/tax/persistence logic under cover of the TS migration.

— UPDATED 2026-06-30 (canonical shadcn-svelte re-platform, ADR-002): the Svelte/TS migration is COMPLETE and the canonical-shadcn re-platform moved the core to `src/lib/core/*.ts` (adapters/core/store/types/format/report/sampledata/demostore/curveseries). The guardrail still holds: the core stays framework-agnostic + node-tested (test-adapters/version/auth/flags/tax/demostore), imported by components as-is — never reimplement adapter parsing or the metrics/cost/tax math in a component.

items.title: Discuss: which Svelte features to utilize
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS. Survey Svelte 5 features we under-use and where they'd help in the runes-first SPA: $derived/$effect patterns, snippets, stores vs. context (we use context('bb:store')), transitions/animations, actions (we have styleProps/modal), lazy/async components, error boundaries, and the compiler a11y warnings. Map each to current/roadmap UI (curve, calendar, the module menu R12, resizable modules R13, blotter F23). Benefit vs. complexity; recommendation + follow-ups. Done when: a written shortlist with recommendations is delivered. — UPDATE 2026-07-01 (backlog review): since filed, transitions/animations landed (A146: fade/fly/slide + FLIP + reduced-motion) and lazy/async component loading landed (screen code-splitting with {#await import()}); the modal.ts action was retired with CH16. Focus any remaining survey on: snippets vs slots usage, $derived.by patterns, attachments/actions, and $props.id() (now used by TagInput).

items.title: Discuss/explore: deeper Svelte 5 runes + reactivity adoption
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS. The app is already runes-only (no `export let` / `$:` / createEventDispatcher; state is `$state` / `$derived` / `$effect`, with cross-component state via `context('bb:store')` + component runes). Survey where it could lean further into Svelte 5's reactivity model for clarity and perf: extracting shared reactive state into `.svelte.ts` rune modules (ONE exists today — src/app/lib/dashboard.svelte.ts, the createDashboard factory) vs the current context approach; `$derived.by` for the heavier App.svelte computed chains; `$effect` cleanup/teardown correctness; `$bindable()` props where parent/child two-way binding is currently faked with callbacks; `$state.snapshot` at the persistence boundaries; and fine-grained vs coarse reactivity in the dashboard. Use the Svelte MCP docs (list-sections / get-documentation) as the reference. For each: benefit vs churn and fit with A28 (deps) / A29 (verbatim core). Recommendation plus any spun-off items. Adjacent: A65 (which Svelte features to utilize). Done when: a written shortlist with recommendations is delivered.

items.title: Verify the 9 beta CSV adapters against real exports
items.prompt: Spun off from the R1 audit (2026-06-29). Only the TradingView adapter is verified; the other adapters in src/lib/core/adapters.ts (Tradovate, Rithmic, SierraChart, TradeStation, MotiveWave, Webull, IBKR, Schwab) are marked beta. Validate each against a REAL export from that platform — symbol/root, side, qty, PnL, date/time, and (for fills exports) the FIFO round-trip pairing + holdMs — and add a representative fixture to scripts/test-adapters.mjs for each verified format. Drop the beta flag per adapter only once validated, and note it in data/changelog.json. Needs real statements (external dependency); ship incrementally, one adapter per pass. Done when: at least one more adapter is verified end-to-end with a fixture and its beta flag cleared. — UPDATE 2026-07-04: owner will supply TradingView's ADDITIONAL export types first (only Balance History verified today — feeds A106), then Quantower and Tradovate/NinjaTrader exports soon after. NOTE Quantower and NinjaTrader are NOT yet supported platforms — new-adapter work is filed as A209; this item stays the verification pass for the existing nine.

items.title: Evaluate supporting TradingView’s other CSV export types
items.prompt: EVALUATION. TradingView can export several CSV shapes (e.g. the Strategy Tester / List-of-trades exports, account-manager history, and the balance/closed-position export the current adapter targets). Inventory which formats exist, which the existing TradingView adapter in src/lib/core/adapters.ts handles, and which are worth supporting. For each candidate: does it map cleanly to the normalized Trade shape (time/date/pnl/symbol/root/side/qty/holdMs), or does it need fills-pairing? Propose the best USER FLOW (auto-detect via sniff() vs an explicit picker in the landing/import UI). Keep compute()/costModel() unchanged (A29 — adapters normalize). Done when: a written evaluation + recommendation (which formats, detection strategy) is delivered, with adapter work spun off as its own item(s). — UPDATE 2026-07-02 (backlog review): overlaps A176 (platform-aware module availability), which explicitly covers the TradingView multi-export question — run the two evaluations together or fold this into A176's design note.

items.title: How-to: add per-platform CSV-export screenshots + clearer steps
items.prompt: Improve the How-To wiki (src/site/components/Howto.svelte) so a new user can reliably export the right CSV from each supported platform. Add per-platform screenshots of the export flow and tighten the step-by-step instructions (where the export lives, which option to pick, common gotchas). Images go through Vite's asset pipeline (src/assets/ or co-located, fingerprinted) and must respect the CSP/img-src; keep the page prerendered (SSG, A69) and the bundle lean (A28/size-budget). Done when: each supported platform has clear instructions + at least one screenshot, and the page builds + prerenders cleanly. — UPDATE 2026-07-04: owner will capture screenshots; in the interim, use other trading-journal products' public import docs/wikis as REFERENCE for accurate per-platform steps, but do NOT republish their screenshots (copyright) — ship only our own captures or officially-licensed platform doc images.

items.title: Discussion/doc: data flow & data-management overview
items.prompt: Write a concise overview (docs/) of how data moves through the app: CSV upload -> Adapters.detect/parse (normalization + fills pairing) -> Store.addTrades delta-merge -> IndexedDB persistence (the trades/journal/meta/trademeta object stores) -> loadRefData ref-data -> applyFilters -> compute/costModel -> view. Clarify what is transformed vs stored raw, the Store seam (real IndexedDB vs in-memory DemoStore), the stable tradeId/dedupe, and the local-compute/no-egress guarantee. Largely documents existing behavior (cross-check against store.ts/adapters.ts/core.ts). Done when: a written data-flow + data-management overview is committed under docs/ (pairs well with A109's diagrams). — UPDATE 2026-07-01 (backlog review): PARTIALLY covered by the PR #91 diagrams (csv-import-adapters, compute-costmodel-render, storage/mode separation). Remaining scope = a single concise PROSE overview stitching the flow end-to-end (or a decision that the diagrams + CLAUDE.md data-flow section suffice and this closes).

items.title: Discussion: free SAST options for the CI pipeline
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/EVALUATION. Survey free static-analysis (SAST) tools we could add to CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) for this stack (TS + Svelte + Cloudflare Functions): e.g. GitHub CodeQL (free for the repo's visibility tier), Semgrep OSS, npm/osv dependency scanning, and any Svelte/TS-aware linters beyond the current ESLint. For each: setup cost, signal/noise, false-positive burden, runtime in CI, and fit with A28 (no heavy/unpinned tooling) and the existing gates. Recommend which (if any) to adopt and how to wire them (a separate workflow vs a CI step, fail vs report). Done when: a written shortlist with a recommendation is delivered (adoption spun off as its own item).

items.title: Evaluate gridstack.js vs a custom build for the resizable tiling grid (CSP/A28)
items.prompt: EVALUATION (spun off from R13; the chosen direction is a TILING dashboard grid, library-vs-custom undecided). Evaluate gridstack.js (and any equivalent framework-agnostic grid lib) for the resizable/snapping dashboard grid against THIS repo's constraints: (1) the `style-src 'self'` CSP (S18/A55) — does the lib inject inline style="" attributes or a <style> element (CSP-VIOLATING), or apply geometry via the CSSOM (element.style.setProperty, which CSP allows)? This is the make-or-break test. (2) A28 dependency discipline — bundle size vs the size-budget (A96), pinned + audited + SRI-able, and the local-compute/no-egress gate (no telemetry). (3) fit with Svelte 5 runes + the CURRENT dashboard substrate (post-CH16: Dashboard.svelte's MODULES/modOrder + animate:flip, the staged-layout Save model (A186), DashTabs, the store.local layout keys + workspace templates — the old Panel system and A108 module registry were deleted) — does it own the DOM in a way that fights Svelte's keyed each/flip?. (4) mobile degradation + a11y (keyboard move/resize). Compare against a CUSTOM minimal grid built on our drag-reorder + styleProps + Store.local primitives. Done when: a written recommendation (gridstack vs custom, with the explicit CSP verdict) + the concrete phase-1 scope for F29 is delivered.

items.title: (staging) Resizable tiling dashboard grid — phase 1: column width + snap
items.prompt:
(STAGING) Resizable tiling dashboard grid — PHASE 1 (spun off from R13; gated on the R20 library-vs-custom eval). First increment toward Quantower-style resizable/snapping modules, scoped tight: make the F26 parallel-grid columns (cal/cost/adv) RESIZABLE by WIDTH — drag a column edge, SNAP to a fixed track grid (e.g. 12-col), neighbors reflow — building on the CURRENT primitives (post-CH16: Dashboard.svelte's MODULES/modOrder single-column stack with menu reorder + animate:flip, the styleProps CSSOM action for CSP-clean dimensions, and the store.local seam — per-tab layout keys via modKeyFor + workspace templates; the old Panel drag and A108 registry were deleted). Persist per-module width per TAB (extend the modKeyFor layout payload from a 1-D key order to {key,span}[]; migrate the old shape) and respect the A186 staged-save model (width drags stage behind the dirty asterisk, Save persists). MUST: degrade to the current single-column stack on narrow/mobile; stay CSP-clean (no inline style="" — geometry via styleProps / CSS grid spans, A55); rAF-throttle the resize drag; keep compute() untouched (resize changes layout, not data); a11y keyboard fallback for resize. Staging-gated (STAGING_PAGE) until promoted (CH16). Later phases (height resize, full snap/reflow, drag-between-cells) are SEPARATE items. Done when: grid columns resize + snap + persist on staging, mobile degrades cleanly, build + npm test + e2e green.

— UPDATED 2026-07-01 (UI-redesign Phase 3 cutover): the target surface moved. The staging dashboard is now the redesigned `src/app/screens/Dashboard.svelte` (inside StagingApp.svelte), which ALREADY has a module system — a `modOrder` $state of stacked, reorderable/hideable/re-addable Card modules (perf/cal/cost/adv), persisted via Store.local under the staging-namespaced `bb:staging:dashModules` key, managed with a DropdownMenu (move up/down, hide, Add module). Build phase 1 on THAT (not the old App.svelte `.modgrid`): make those stacked modules resizable by width + snap to a track grid, and extend the persisted layout (currently a 1-D key order) to carry per-module width. Keep the CSP-clean geometry rule (no inline style="" — use a CSSOM/styleProps action, A55), rAF-throttle, mobile single-column degrade, compute() untouched. R20 (library-vs-custom) still gates the approach. — UPDATE 2026-07-01 (backlog review): PREMISE DRIFTED — the F26 parallel-grid columns were replaced by the CH16 redesign (the dashboard is now a stacked full-width module list under src/app/screens/Dashboard.svelte, plus staging-only tabs per A135). Re-scope phase 1 against the CURRENT module list (e.g. per-module width on a 12-col grid) after the R20 library-vs-custom eval; coordinate with A142's module brainstorm.

items.title: CI optimization: parallel lint/typecheck job + npm run ci lockstep
items.prompt: OPTIONAL optimization from the 2026-07-01 R1 audit (CI is already correct + de-duped per A96). Two low-risk wins: (1) split the cheap gates (lint + typecheck + format:check) into a separate GitHub Actions job that runs in PARALLEL with the build+e2e job, to cut wall-clock feedback time (the e2e build is the long pole). Keep the npm/Playwright caches. (2) Add a single top-level `npm run ci` script that chains `test` + `build` + `size-budget` + `check-deploy` + `test:e2e` so CI and local stay in lockstep (CI currently open-codes the sequence). Neither is a correctness bug; ship only if the CI latency/lockstep is worth it. Done when: applied (or explicitly declined with a note).

items.title: (staging) Local/remote toggle + scaffolding for synced workspaces
items.prompt: UNBLOCKED 2026-07-01 — A138 landed (docs/vault-storage-assessment.md): the workspace selector should read Local (this browser) / Vault (folder — future, Chromium-only per the assessment) / Remote (stub), with the vault Phase-1 export/import as the first implementable slice. On the staging surface (gated behind isStaging/PAGE_MODE==='staging', demo stays non-mutating), add a Local/Remote workspace toggle in the UI plus the scaffolding for future synced workspaces: a settings/toggle control, a persisted preference (via the Store seam, not direct storage), and a stubbed 'remote' path that no-ops or shows 'coming soon' until the cloud tier exists. Do NOT implement real sync or any trade-data egress — the moat holds (compute stays local). Reuse the existing StoreLike seam (context('bb:store')) so a future CloudStore drops in unchanged. Done when: the toggle renders + persists on staging, the remote path is safely stubbed, and demo/prod are unaffected. — TABLED by the owner 2026-07-01: revisit together with remote-sync planning (the toggle and the vault Phase-1 slice ride along when that thread opens). Not a candidate for near-term scheduling.

items.title: Discussion: performance optimizations
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/EVALUATION. Survey concrete optimization opportunities across the app now that the redesign has grown the /app/ bundle (516.8 KiB / 600 KiB budget, ~83 KiB headroom). Cover: bundle size (code-split heavy screens/deps, lazy-load rarely-used surfaces like Reports export, tree-shaking bits-ui/lucide imports), compute() re-run cost on large trade sets (memoization/$derived granularity), IndexedDB read patterns on boot, chart/curve render cost, and any redundant $effect work. For each: rough win vs effort/risk, and whether it fits the size-budget gate. Recommend a prioritized shortlist; spin adopted ones off as their own items. Done when: a written, prioritized optimization shortlist is delivered. — UPDATE 2026-07-01 (backlog review): the headline suggestion LANDED — the non-default screens are code-split with idle prefetch. MEASUREMENT CORRECTED 2026-07-02 (A190): the gate now sums boot + lazy chunks — true app total 559.4/600 KiB (~41 KiB headroom; boot ~411 KiB first paint). Remaining candidates: the utils chunk (~124 KiB — bits-ui/tailwind-merge), main chunk trimming, anB headroom). Remaining candidates for this discussion: the bits-ui select/dialog chunk weight, Geist Mono subsetting, compute() memo/pruning for very large fills imports, and whether the 600 KiB budget should ratchet back down (e.g. 500) now that headroom exists.

items.title: Discussion: add contract expiry / delivery month info
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/EVALUATION. Futures symbols carry a delivery/expiry month (e.g. ESZ4 = Dec 2024). Explore surfacing contract expiry-month info: is it derivable from the raw export symbols the adapters already see (before rootSym() strips to the root), or would it need a contract-code parser (month letter F/G/H/… + year digit)? Where would it add value — per-trade detail, blotter column, or expiry-aware grouping/rollover analysis? Consider adapter coverage (which platforms include the full contract code) and the normalized Trade shape (add an optional `contract`/`expiry` field without breaking compute/costModel). Done when: a written recommendation on whether/how to capture and surface expiry month, with any implementation spun off as its own item.

items.title: (Discussion) Changelog email signup + notify on changelog update
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/EVALUATION. Explore letting users subscribe to changelog (Blotterlog) emails and sending a notification whenever data/changelog.json gains a prod entry. This needs a backend/list mechanism the current static-site + edge-functions stack doesn't have: evaluate options (a Cloudflare Pages Function + KV/D1 for the list + a transactional email provider, or a third-party newsletter service) and the privacy/opt-in/unsubscribe + double-opt-in requirements. HARD constraint: this touches ONLY email addresses + changelog content — never any trade/journal data (the moat holds). Consider how the send would trigger (on the prod version bump / changelog commit). Recommend an approach + rough cost; spin implementation off as its own item. Done when: a written recommendation on signup + send mechanism (incl. privacy) is delivered.

items.title: Discussion: ideas for more dashboards / dashboard modules
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/BRAINSTORM. Propose additional dashboards or dashboard modules that would add value for futures traders, grounded in the metrics the pure-logic core already computes (compute()/costModel()/curveseries — e.g. drawdown, expectancy, streaks, session/day-of-week splits, cost/tax breakdown, per-symbol stats) plus any that need new (cheap, local) computation. For each idea: what it shows, which existing metrics it uses, rough effort, and whether it's a new dashboard vs a module in the existing grid. Prioritize a shortlist. Done when: a written, prioritized list of candidate dashboards/modules is delivered; adopted ones spun off as their own items. — UPDATE 2026-07-02 (backlog review): the delivery surface is ready — dashboard tabs ship on all surfaces with per-tab layouts, and the A189 add-modules picker (with per-module SVG thumbnails) makes new modules discoverable; a new module = a MODULES entry + snippet in Dashboard.svelte + a picker thumbnail.

items.title: (Discussion) Platform-aware module availability — richer CSVs unlock more calculations
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/DESIGN first, then build. Different platform exports carry different data richness: fills exports (Tradovate/IBKR/...) yield qty/entry-exit/hold time while close-event exports (TradingView balance history) don't — today the UI just renders '—' where data is missing. Design an 'available modules/calculations' capability model: each adapter declares what it provides (e.g. capabilities: ['holdTime','qty','entryExit','realized']), the import records the active dataset's capability union, and modules/stat rows that need a missing capability either hide or render an explicit 'not in your platform's export' state instead of silent dashes. Cover the TradingView case explicitly: TV can export MULTIPLE different CSVs (balance history, the 'List of trades' paper-trading export, and per-account variants) with different columns — decide the most elegant handling (separate adapters per TV export flavor sharing a family label vs one adapter with per-flavor capability detection; the import preview should say WHICH TV export it detected and what it unlocks). Prereq/related: F37 (per-file provenance) would let capabilities be tracked per file rather than per dataset. Deliverable: a short design note in docs/ + the capability map in adapters.ts + gating in the affected modules. Done when: importing a data-poor CSV visibly explains what's unavailable and why, and each TV export flavor detects + labels correctly.

items.title: CSV upload security hardening (type/size/content validation at every intake)
items.prompt: All parsing is client-side (no server to compromise) but the intake should still be hardened against hostile/degenerate files. Add a shared validation layer in front of Adapters.parse at ALL CSV intake points — CsvLibrary upload zone + file picker, the Onboarding dropzone, and any drag-drop path: (1) extension/MIME allowlist (.csv/.txt, text/*) with a clear rejection message; (2) size cap (e.g. 20 MB) and row cap with a friendly 'file too large' error instead of a frozen tab; (3) binary sniff (NUL bytes / non-text control chars → reject as not-a-CSV); (4) keep and extend the existing content guards — rootSym charset restriction (XSS via symbol), CSV formula escaping on EXPORT (A154), SHOT_RE validation for images; (5) a fuzz fixture in scripts/test-adapters.mjs feeding junk (binary blob, 10k-column line, deeply quoted garbage) asserting graceful ok:false errors, never exceptions. Keep the moat: validation must be pure client-side, no file ever leaves the browser. Related: A178 (approved-platform gate). Done when: every intake path rejects oversized/binary/wrong-type files with a readable message and the fuzz fixtures are green.

items.title: Reject CSVs that don't come from an approved platform (strict detection gate)
items.prompt: Adapters.detect currently picks the best-scoring sniff > 0 — a random CSV that happens to contain a keyword can land in the wrong adapter and misparse (A174 tightened this with word-boundary matching, but weak single-point matches can still win). Add a strictness gate: (1) define a minimum confidence score per adapter (sniff scores are already weighted 3-5 for distinctive headers) and refuse anything below it; (2) on refusal, show an explicit 'This CSV doesn't match a supported platform export' error listing the nine supported platforms and linking the Howto import guides, instead of importing garbage; (3) make the import preview always name the detected platform + its beta status so a wrong detection is visible BEFORE confirm (partially exists — assert it); (4) fixtures: a generic bank-statement-ish CSV and a shuffled-header file must both refuse; every real platform fixture must still detect. Related: A177 (intake hardening), A176 (capability model). Done when: no unrecognized CSV can silently import through a low-scoring adapter match.

items.title: Discussion: Claude Code skills to accelerate this project's development
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS. We already have repo-specific skills (.claude/skills — e.g. promote-staging for the CH16 staging→prod ritual, repo-audit for R1). Survey what ADDITIONAL Claude Code skills would speed up recurring development work in this repo and reduce error-prone manual steps. Candidates to weigh: adding a platform CSV adapter (the adapters.ts object + test-adapters.mjs fixture ritual), adding a dashboard module (MODULES entry + Dashboard.svelte snippet + A189 picker thumbnail), adding a shadcn-svelte primitive (vendor-by-hand since the CLI registry is egress-blocked), a rate-data update + build-manifest run, adding a new backlog item in this exact format, or a release/changelog helper. For each: what the skill would automate, the manual pain it removes, rough effort to author it (skill-creator), and priority. Done when: a written, prioritized shortlist of candidate skills is delivered; adopted ones are authored via anthropic-skills:skill-creator and spun off as their own items.

items.title: Big analytics dashboard review — add interactivity, filters & functionality to modules
items.prompt: Full review + upgrade of the Analytics screen (src/app/screens/Analytics.svelte, view-model in src/app/lib/analytics.ts). Today the analytics modules are largely static read-outs; make them interactive. Audit every module and add: per-module and/or page-level filters (date range, symbol/root, session, side, win/loss) that reuse the existing filter model where possible; drill-down / hover detail on charts; sort/paginate on tabular modules; toggles for grouping (by day-of-week, session, symbol) grounded in what compute()/costModel()/curveseries already produce (don't add server compute — moat is client-only). Reuse installed shadcn-svelte primitives (tabs/select/popover/tooltip/table) and the chart-1..5 tokens; keep CSP style-src 'self' (no inline style). Coordinate with A142 (new module ideas) and F29/R20 (resizable grid). Start by delivering a written audit of each current module with a proposed interactivity upgrade + effort, then implement the shortlist. Done when: the analytics modules support filtering/drill-down/sorting as specced, demo stays non-mutating, and e2e + size-budget gates stay green.

items.title: Auto-name new dashboard tabs "New tab N" instead of prompting for a name
items.prompt: Small UX fix. createDashTab() in src/app/App.svelte (~line 183) currently opens a blocking prompt('New dashboard tab name…') and aborts tab creation if the user cancels/enters nothing. Change it to create the tab immediately with an auto-generated default name — 'New tab N' where N makes the name unique among existing dashTabs (e.g. lowest unused index, or dashTabs.length+1) — no prompt. The existing renameDashTab() flow (the DashTabs menu → Rename) already lets the user rename afterward, so keep that untouched. Preserve the rest of createDashTab (id generation, selectDashTab persist, the emit('tab:created') activity-log line). Done when: clicking '+ New tab' instantly adds a uniquely-named 'New tab N' with no dialog, and rename still works.

items.title: (Discussion) Audit our mobile approach — is it best-practice or does it need re-architecting?
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS (no code). Assess how the app currently handles mobile across the Svelte SPA: the AppShell slide-over drawer nav (src/lib/components/shell/), the responsive Tailwind breakpoints used across screens/parts, the dashboard tabs + module grid on narrow viewports, tables (Blotter/Analytics) on small screens, and any touch-vs-pointer gaps. Compare against current best practice for a data-dense trading dashboard on mobile (responsive-adaptive vs a distinct mobile layout, touch targets, horizontal-scroll tables vs card views, gesture support). Call out where we're fine, where we're papering over problems, and whether a deliberate mobile architecture (per-surface layouts, container queries, a mobile-first component pass) is warranted vs incremental fixes. Related: A200 (mobile module card-stack), user cares about wide/fullscreen too (M4 Mac Studio). Done when: a written assessment with a clear recommendation (keep-and-refine vs re-architect) and a prioritized follow-up list is delivered; adopted items spun off.

items.title: Mobile: swipeable card-stack for the 5 top dashboard modules (one at a time, arrows/swipe)
items.prompt: On mobile, replace the vertically-stacked top-five dashboard modules (the summary/stat modules at the top of src/app/screens/Dashboard.svelte) with a single card-stack component that shows ONE module at a time and lets the user swipe left/right (touch) or tap left/right arrow controls to move between them, with a dot/index indicator. It's a cleaner, less scroll-heavy view on small screens. Constraints: mobile only — keep the existing multi-module layout at desktop breakpoints (render the stack under a Tailwind breakpoint / container query, the grid above); reuse chart-1..5 tokens and shadcn-svelte primitives; support keyboard (arrow keys) + reduced-motion (A146 pattern); CSP style-src 'self' (no inline style — use the styleProps action for any dynamic transform). Consider a small reusable ModuleCarousel component so other screens can reuse it. Related: A199 (mobile audit). Done when: on a mobile viewport the top modules render as a swipeable/arrow-navigable one-at-a-time stack, desktop is unchanged, and e2e + size-budget gates stay green.

items.title: Wire up /api/geo — prefill the tax-estimate US state from the visitor's edge region
items.prompt: The Cloudflare Pages Function functions/api/geo.ts already returns the visitor's coarse { country, region, regionCode } from request.cf (private-cached, no IP stored), but NOTHING in src/ calls it. Wire it into the app: on the cost/tax setup path (CostSetup part / state-tax selection), fetch('/api/geo'), and when country==='US' pre-select the matching state (regionCode → state-tax.json entry) as a convenience default the user can still change. Constraints: purely a convenience prefill — never block on the request, tolerate failure/offline/non-US silently (no error UI), and don't call it in demo if it adds noise (or guard by PAGE_MODE if appropriate). Keep the moat: the request carries no trade data; it only reads coarse geo. Done when: a US visitor sees their state pre-selected in the tax estimate, the fetch fails gracefully everywhere else, and demo stays non-mutating.

items.title: Add an arbitration clause to the legal terms
items.prompt: Add a binding-arbitration / dispute-resolution clause to the terms in src/site/components/Legal.svelte (legal.html; prerendered by scripts/vite-ssg.mjs). Cover the usual: agreement to arbitrate disputes, the governing arbitration body/rules, venue/governing law, a class-action waiver, and any opt-out window — sized to a client-side, no-data-leaves-the-browser trading journal. This ties into F18 (pre-monetization legal pack: ToS/Privacy/Disclaimer) and CH22 (footer operator/entity line) — keep entity naming consistent with whatever's decided there. NOTE: exact legal wording is an owner/counsel decision — draft reasonable placeholder language and flag it as needing legal review before ship, don't present it as final legal advice. Done when: the terms include a clearly-sectioned arbitration clause (with class-action waiver + opt-out), consistent with the rest of the legal pack, flagged for owner/legal sign-off.

items.title: "Commission Compare" module/table — compare commission rates across brokers
items.prompt: Build a 'Commission Compare' module/table that lets a user compare commission rates across brokers side-by-side, sourced from data/brokers.json (commission tiers) + the exchange/clearing/NFA fees in data/exchange-fees.json (all fetched via loadRefData with the pure-logic cost model). Let the user pick a contract/root and (optionally) a monthly volume/tier, then show a sortable table of per-broker all-in per-side/round-turn cost (commission + exchange + clearing + NFA), highlighting the cheapest. Reuse the costModel() math so the numbers reconcile with the dashboard; use the shadcn-svelte table + select primitives and chart tokens. Decide placement: a new analytics/dashboard module (coordinate with A142) or a standalone screen. Watch F30 (effective-dated historical rates) and R21 (brokers↔exchange-fees relationship) for the data model. Done when: users can compare all-in commissions across brokers for a chosen contract/volume in a sortable table that matches costModel() output; demo works with sample data; gates stay green.

items.title: Discussion: evaluate modern-feel UI libraries (anime.js, Kokonut UI, bklit UI)
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/EVALUATION. Assess libraries that could make the app feel more modern/polished, specifically anime.js (JS animation engine), Kokonut UI, and bklit UI (component/animation collections) — plus note any obvious peers. For EACH: what it offers, how it fits our stack (Svelte 5 runes + Tailwind v4 + shadcn-svelte/bits-ui), license, bundle-size cost against the 600 KiB /app/ budget (A96/A136), and CSP compatibility (style-src 'self' — no inline style; motion via CSSOM/styleProps action, A55). Note that Svelte transitions + FLIP + tw-animate-css already landed (A146) and shadcn-svelte owns our primitives — so frame each as 'what does it add beyond what we have, and where'. Recommendation per library: adopt / selectively borrow patterns / skip. Done when: a written comparison + recommendation is delivered; anything adopted is spun off as its own item.

items.title: Add tooltips to icon/ambiguous buttons across the app
items.prompt: Polish pass. The shadcn-svelte tooltip primitive is installed ($lib/components/ui/tooltip) but isn't used in src/app. Add tooltips to buttons whose purpose isn't obvious from a visible label — icon-only buttons (sidebar toggle, dashboard tab close/menu, module controls, blotter row actions, reorder/save/dirty affordances). Use the canonical Tooltip (portals to body; keyboard/focus + hover accessible) with concise text; don't tooltip buttons that already have clear text labels. Keep it consistent (delay, side) and CSP-safe. Consider a small wrapper/snippet if the pattern repeats. Related: A199/A200 (mobile — tooltips are hover-only, so ensure touch users still get the affordance). Done when: icon/ambiguous buttons across the app surfaces have accessible tooltips, e2e stays green, and no inline style is introduced.

items.title: Add skeleton loaders for boot/async surfaces
items.prompt: Polish pass. The shadcn-svelte skeleton primitive is installed ($lib/components/ui/skeleton) but isn't used in src/app. Add skeleton placeholders on the surfaces that briefly show empty/blank while data resolves — dashboard modules/curve during boot (loadRefData → Store.init → restoreSession), the code-split lazy screens ({#await import()} fallbacks: Analytics/Reports/etc.), and any async table load. Match the real layout's shape/spacing so there's no layout shift when content arrives; respect reduced-motion (A146); keep CSP style-src 'self'. Don't add artificial delay — only show skeletons while genuinely pending. Done when: boot and lazy-screen loads show shape-matched skeletons instead of blank/flash, no CLS regression, and gates stay green.

items.title: Discussion: static-loading / prerendering the app surface for faster first paint
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS. The marketing/info site already prerenders to static HTML at build time (A69 SSG via scripts/vite-ssg.mjs), but the /app/ surfaces (app/demo/staging) are bare mount points that render entirely client-side after the JS bundle loads (boot ~411 KiB first paint per A190). Explore options to make the app feel like it 'static loads': a prerendered app shell / above-the-fold skeleton in the HTML (pairs with A206), inlining critical layout, streaming/priority hints, or partial SSG of the static chrome — WITHOUT breaking the hard constraints (client-only compute, no trade data server-side, IndexedDB-driven state, CSP style-src 'self'). For each option: expected first-paint/perceived-perf win, complexity/risk, and interaction with the size budget (A136) and the no-SvelteKit decision (A62). Done when: a written, prioritized set of recommendations is delivered; adopted ones spun off as their own items.

items.title: Use real per-trade commissions from the CSV to override modeled commissions
items.prompt: Some platform CSVs carry ACTUAL commission/fee amounts per row (e.g. IBKR's ibCommission — see the fills comment in src/lib/core/adapters.ts; others may include commission/fees columns). Today costModel() (src/lib/core/core.ts) estimates commissions purely from brokers.json + exchange-fees.json (tier x volume x root), ignoring any real cost in the file. Change this so that when a trade's source CSV includes real commission data, that actual amount OVERRIDES the modeled commission for those specific trades, while trades WITHOUT file-provided commissions keep using the model. Scope: (1) capture the exported commission/fee on the normalized shape — add an optional `commission` (and consider fees) to the Trade interface (src/lib/core/types.ts) and to Fill, and have each adapter that exposes it populate it in toTrades()/pairFills() (start with IBKR; make it generic so other adapters can opt in); (2) in costModel(), for trades that carry a real commission use it verbatim, else fall back to the tier model — and reflect the mix in the commission breakdown (bySym) so totals reconcile; (3) surface it in the UI (cost setup / dashboard) so the user knows some rows use real broker costs vs modeled estimates, and — decide with the owner — whether a global toggle 'prefer CSV commissions when present' is warranted. Keep the pure-logic core node-tested: add a fixture asserting a commission-bearing CSV yields file commissions and a bare CSV yields modeled ones. Related: F30 (effective-dated rates), A177/A178 (CSV intake), A203 (Commission Compare). Done when: trades imported with real commission data cost out at the file's actual commissions, model-only trades are unchanged, totals reconcile, and the distinction is visible in the UI. — DECIDED 2026-07-04 (owner): ALWAYS-OVERRIDE with a visible per-trade/summary indicator; NO global toggle. Implement together with F37 (one coordinated Trade-shape + Store migration).

items.title: New CSV adapters: Quantower + NinjaTrader
items.prompt: Add platform adapters for Quantower and NinjaTrader — neither is among the nine supported platforms in src/lib/core/adapters.ts. Owner has accounts on both and will supply REAL exports (blocked until then — don't guess header shapes from docs alone). Per the 'Adding things' recipe: one adapter object each (sniff + toTrades) normalizing to the standard Trade shape { time, date, pnl, symbol, root, side[, qty, entryTime, exitTime, holdMs] } (use pairFills() if the export is fills-based), a representative fixture in scripts/test-adapters.mjs per format, beta-flagged until verified (A103 pattern). Mind the A178 strict-detection gate: each new sniff must clear its minimum-confidence bar on real files and not misfire on the other platforms' fixtures. Update the Howto import guide (A107) with each platform's export steps. Done when: both adapters detect + parse real exports with fixtures green, ship beta-flagged, and the supported-platform list/docs are updated.

items.title: Rates upkeep: verify 2026 CME fee changes in our composites + opportunistic history backfill
items.prompt: Follow-up from F30 (see docs/rates-data-assessment.md — Follow-ups). (1) CME published a 2026-02-01 fee schedule AND announced further transaction-fee changes effective 2026-04-01; our current composites in static/data/exchange-fees.json are a 'mid-2026 snapshot' (A171, 2026-07-02) that SHOULD include both — spot-check the equity/energy/metals/micro groups against cmegroup.com's current schedule (clearing-fees page); if anything moved, bump the current values and push the prior values into a new history entry ('until': '2026-03-31' or '2026-01-31' as documented), then run build-manifest.mjs. (2) Backfill deeper (pre-2024) exchange-fee history from cmegroup.com/company/clearing-fees/historical-fees.html only if users import multi-year-old data. (3) Add broker rateHistory entries ONLY with a citable source (broker announcement or a Wayback capture of their pricing page) — never fabricate. Schema needs no changes for any of this. Done when: the 2026 spot-check is recorded (values confirmed or corrected+dated) and any added history entries carry citations.

howToDrive: One item per prompt; one surface per prompt where possible.
Lead with the *goal*, not the mechanism; let me pick the workable path.
If you batch, name the 1–3 must-haves in case I run low on budget.
Keep using the change-plan + memory pattern for any multi-step item.
